items.title: Viva Energy boss says more refineries needed for secure fuel supply

children.content: The Viva Energy refinery provides about 10 per cent of Australia's fuel. Its chief, Scott Wyatt, chats to Alan Kohler about the company's future in the wake of the Geelong fire.

items.title: How Aussies near you are coping with the fuel crisis

children.content: Petrol prices are reshaping Australian life in ways big and small — from a grandmother's isolation to a farmer's future.

image.altText: Three people who are affected by high fuel prices.

items.title: Daylesford intersection where five people died to become green space

children.content: An intersection in a country Victorian town where five people died will be closed after $1 million in funding was offered as part of the Black Spots Program.

image.altText: A pub with tables and umbrellas in the front, with a large tree creating shade.

items.title: The legacy of once-banned soldiers that hides in plain sight

children.content: Thousands of Indian soldiers served alongside Anzac forces at Gallipoli, leaving their legacy in the "puggaree" — a traditional Indian headwrap that was adapted to adorn the slouch hats worn by the Australian army.

gallery.altText: Six men with red and white striped turbans in khaki uniforms with red and white around their waist and long white socks.

thumbnail.altText: Six men with red and white striped turbans in khaki uniforms with red and white around their waist and long white socks.

avatar.alt: Sacha Payne, dressed in a red jacket and white shirt, stands outside Victorian Parliament.

items.title: Collard's 'crippling' ban for homophobic slur reduced on appeal

children.content: St Kilda successfully appeals the AFL's nine-week ban handed to forward Lance Collard over a homophobic slur, reducing it to four weeks with two suspended.

image.altText: Lance Collard high fives St Kilda fans

items.title: Bulldogs offer update after star's horror fall as Curnow kicks a bag

children.content: Sydney's hold on top spot is strengthened by a 66-point win over the Western Bulldogs, whose injury woes continue.

image.altText: nauigthon

items.title: Ancient fossils reveal when the iconic Twelve Apostles formed

children.content: An analysis of tiny fossils of sea creatures found in rock samples around the Port Campbell region in south-western Victoria has refined our understanding of how the Twelve Apostles formed.

gallery.altText: Four limestone monoliths of varying size lapped at by shoreline waves next to massive cliffs.

thumbnail.altText: Four limestone monoliths of varying size lapped at by shoreline waves next to massive cliffs.

avatar.alt: A man in a blue checked shirt and blue jacket with a beard and long hair.

items.title: New grapevine virus GINV detected for first time in Australia

children.content: A grapevine virus known in China and Japan that affects plant growth and productivity has been detected in Australia for the first time in Victorian, Queensland and South Australian table grapes.

image.altText: Undergrowth of table grape vines.

items.title: Wyndham mayor refuses to resign after sex offender character reference

children.content: Embattled mayor Preet Singh says he will return to the role after stepping aside earlier this month amid revelations that he provided a character reference to a convicted child sex offender.

image.altText: A man in mayoral ceremonial robes poses for a professional portrait.

items.title: Coroner refers Byron death of Melbourne van-packer to homicide police

children.content: The NSW state coroner recommends the death of van-packer Jackson Stacker near Byron Bay be referred to the Unsolved Homicide Squad.

image.altText: Man with dreadlocks at the beach

items.title: 'Bordering on bullying': Voss hits out at coverage of Hollands

children.content: Michael Voss has called out the media for the way it has covered Elijah Hollands's medical episode, saying the coverage of the 23-year-old "bordered on bullying".

image.altText: Michael Voss and Elijah Hollands stand next to each other

items.title: Sheraz risked his life translating in Afghanistan. His family paid the price

children.content: Sheraz worked with Australian and US troops in Afghanistan, as a result his family were targeted by the Taliban. This Anzac Day he's being heroed.

gallery.altText: A man seated at a table, smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A man seated at a table, smiling.

items.title: Thousands sign petition against McDonald's Phillip Island restaurant plan

children.content: The fast food giant is facing backlash after lodging a planning permit application to build a new restaurant in tourist hotspot Cowes.

image.altText: Artist render of a McDonald's restaurant.

items.title: Tears for Ugle-Hagan as ex-Bulldog prepares to make Suns debut

children.content: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan endured a year from hell in 2025, but things are looking up for him this season as he prepares to make his Gold Coast debut.

image.altText: Jamarra Ugle-Hagan smiles

items.title: They answered the call, then Australia turned them away. But one digger fights on

children.content: No family in the world comes close to the fighting dynasty of Ricky Morris's. So, why are they still denied land owed generations ago?

gallery.altText: A man with medals stands on a wrecked church site

items.title: Case dropped against actor accused of 'breaking the girl code'

children.content: The actor was accused of grabbing the breast of a staffer at Parliament House in Melbourne during a pro-Palestine demonstration two years ago.

items.title: New CFA and forest firefighting funding announced

children.content: The Victorian government has unveiled its latest investment in CFA and forest firefighting upgrades.

image.altText: A fire truck is parked out the front of a fire station that was built in 1931.

items.title: 15yo boy dies in collision involving e-motorbike in Melbourne's north

children.content: A teenager died after being thrown from an unregistered electric motorcycle at South Morang on Wednesday evening.

image.altText: Two police officers standing near a bike.

items.title: 'Ghosted': Musicians claim gig booker owes thousands of dollars

children.content: A triple j hack investigation uncovers allegations that a booking agency has failed to pay more than 20 Australian bands and artists.

image.altText: Man in white shirt and black cap plays guitar and sings into microphone.

items.title: 'Be honest with people': Politicians across the country react to NDIS cuts

children.content: State leaders are reacting to the sweeping cuts coming for the National Disability Insurance Scheme, with one minister arguing the federal government will "walk away" from those in need.

image.altText: Amanda Camm wearing a suit jacket and speaking into a microphone.

items.title: How three different households make mealtimes easier

children.content: From share houses to solo living, this is how three households manage the cooking differently.

image.altText: Gemma Plunkett preparing a meal in her light-filled apartment kitchen.

items.title: Meet the eco-venture turning an invasive pest into dog treats

children.content: Introducing the European carp to Australian waterways is an ecological disaster that continues to worsen, but an Adelaide couple have found a novel use for the pest.

image.altText: Young woman feeds a kelpie a treat

items.title: Legal action considered after Dezi Freeman property entry

children.content: Property owner considers legal action after people enter the Upper Murray property where Dezi Freeman was shot.

articles.title: Dezi Freeman's shipping container entered by YouTubers, police investigate

children.content: YouTubers are being investigated for trespass after filming inside the converted shipping container where fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman hid after shooting dead two police officers.

image.altText: Items inside a shipping container.

gallery.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

thumbnail.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Ballarat man arrested at the wheel while on bail for multiple charges

children.content: A man released on bail for about 60 charges, including two counts of culpable driving causing death, has been arrested at the wheel near Ballarat.

image.altText: A photo of two police officers standing next to each other in high-vis. Their heads are not in the photo.

items.title: The growing problem stopping Melbourne residents using their microwaves

children.content: A power company warns that growing electricity demand is creating an undervoltage issue as Victoria's infrastructure struggles to keep up with the transition from gas.

image.altText: Powerlines about a suburban street

items.title: How young boys helped Australians wage guerilla war against Japan in Timor

children.content: Incredible bonds were formed between Australian commandos and Timorese boys during World War II. The White Australia policy meant they had to say goodbye when the Anzacs departed.

image.altText: A graphic shows a contemporary colour of a landscape in Timor with a black and white photo of a boy and soldier in the middle.

items.title: South Sydney might never get a better chance to end the longest NRL hoodoo

children.content: The Rabbitohs have never won a game in Melbourne in almost three decades of trying but they might never get a better chance than this weekend.

image.altText: A mascot in a superhero-type padded costume holds up a toy rabbit before a rugby league match.

items.title: Bus replacements mean some V/Line commuters can't get to work on time

children.content: Some regional commuters hoping to save on petrol are stuck in the slow lane as Victoria's free public transport offer coincides with rail maintenance along the Warrnambool/Geelong line.

children.content: Ticketholders are still waiting for refunds and answers from The Drop music festival.

items.title: Victoria Police Freedom of Information requests face record delays

children.content: Victoria Police is processing Freedom of Information requests slower than at any point in its history, with the wait exceeding nine months.

image.altText: A close up of a woman's hand shuffling though papers.

items.title: If you think a river is for boaties, that's far from the reality in this region

children.content: The first-ever review into the Murray-Darling Basin Plan is underway, and the St George community wants a better deal.

image.altText: Gomeroi elders Ron Waters and Sam Saunders sit on top of the E.J Beardmore Dam near St George, Queensland, August 2023.

items.title: Milk, bread prices to increase within weeks due to war in Iran

children.content: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

image.altText: A woman shopping in a grocery store aisle.

items.title: Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface' after Hollands episode

children.content: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

articles.title: 'I know who my son is': Hollands's father posts heartfelt message

children.content: Elijah Hollands's father, former AFL player Ben Hollands, posts on social media pledging to help lift his son up and urging others to check on their friends if they are struggling.

image.altText: Elijah Hollands high fives AFL fans after a Carlton game.
